declarations.name: analyze_repos
declarations.docComment:
Builds the dependency graph and runs cycle/wildcard analysis, tolerating cycles
(reports rather than throws). The shared core of `gitops_analyze` and
`gitops_validate`, which format the result themselves — so this stays silent
(no `log`) to avoid emitting a redundant "Analyzing dependencies" line.

modules.moduleComment:
Shared dependency graph validation logic used across multiple workflows.

Consolidates graph building, cycle detection, and publishing order computation
that was duplicated in three places: `multi_repo_publisher.ts`, `publishing_plan.ts`,
and `gitops_analyze.task.ts`.

Options pattern supports different behaviors: analyze can tolerate cycles for
reporting, while publish must throw on production cycles.

See also: `dependency_graph.ts` for core graph data structure and algorithms.

declarations.name: LocalRepo
declarations.docComment:
Fully loaded local repo with `Library` and extracted dependency data.
Does not extend `LocalRepoPath` - `Library` is source of truth for name/repo_url/etc.

members.name: package_json
members.docComment: The repo's full `package.json` (with `dependencies`/`devDependencies`).

declarations.name: LocalRepoPath
declarations.docComment:
A repo that has been located on the filesystem (path exists).
Used before loading - just filesystem/git concerns.

declarations.name: LocalRepoMissing
declarations.docComment: A repo that is missing from the filesystem (needs cloning).

declarations.name: local_repo_load
declarations.docComment:
Loads repo data, optionally syncing the working tree first.

When `sync` is `false` (the default for read-only diagnostics like
`gitops_analyze`/`gitops_plan`), the repo is loaded exactly as it sits on
disk — no branch switch, pull, install, or clean-workspace check. This makes
those commands safe to run on an active workspace with uncommitted changes or
feature branches checked out.

When `sync` is `true` (used by `gitops_sync`), the working tree is brought in
line with the configured branch first:
1. Records current commit hash (for detecting changes)
2. Switches to target branch if needed (requires clean workspace unless `allow_dirty`)
3. Pulls latest changes from remote (skipped for local-only repos)
4. Validates workspace is clean after pull (skipped if `allow_dirty`)
5. Auto-installs dependencies if `package.json` changed

Either way it then:
6. Loads `library_json` via `library_load_from_repo` (svelte-docinfo analysis)
7. Creates `Library` and extracts dependency maps
declarations.typeSignature: ({ local_repo_path, log: _log, git_ops, npm_ops, sync, allow_dirty, }: { local_repo_path: LocalRepoPath; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; sync?: boolean | undefined; allow_dirty?: boolean | undefined; }): Promise<...>

throws.description: syncing fails (dirty workspace, branch switch, install) or analysis fails

members.name: events
members.docComment: The structured event stream for this run, in emission order.

members.name: plan_warnings
members.docComment: Non-blocking plan warnings (e.g. the fixed-point iteration limit).

declarations.name: execute_publishing_plan
declarations.docComment:
Executes a frozen publishing plan in a single linear pass — the "dumb executor" half of
the zap model. The plan is the single source of truth; this re-derives nothing.

Fails loud when the plan couldn't be fully computed (`plan.errors`): a wetrun aborts
before any side effect, a dry run still reports the partial cascade but returns
`ok: false`. This is the single error gate — `--no-plan` can't bypass it.
declarations.typeSignature: (repos: LocalRepo[], plan: PublishingPlan, options: PublishingOptions): Promise<PublishingResult>

declarations.name: group_dependency_updates
declarations.docComment:
Groups dependency updates by dependent package — `dependent → (dependency → new
version)`, the shape `update_package_json` consumes. Restricted by `predicate` (e.g.
prod/peer for a given package, or all dev deps) and to dependencies that actually
published this run, so a failed/aborted publish never propagates to its dependents.
declarations.typeSignature: (updates: DependencyUpdate[], published: Map<string, PublishedVersion>, predicate: (update: DependencyUpdate) => boolean): Map<...>

declarations.name: check_package_available

declarations.name: wait_for_package
declarations.docComment:
Waits for package version to propagate to NPM registry.

Uses exponential backoff with jitter to avoid hammering registry.
Logs progress every 5 attempts. Respects timeout to avoid infinite waits.

Critical for multi-repo publishing: ensures published packages are available
before updating dependent packages.
declarations.typeSignature: (pkg: string, version: string, options?: WaitOptions): Promise<void>

throws.description: timeout reached or max attempts exceeded

declarations.docComment:

Returns name and latest version. Returns null if package doesn't exist
or registry is unreachable.

declarations.returnDescription: package info or null on error/not found

File system operations for reading and writing files.

Errors are typed via `FsError` (`not_found | permission_denied |
already_exists | io_error`) so callers can branch on `kind` instead of
regex-matching `message`. See `@fuzdev/fuz_util/fs.js`.

Combined operations interface grouping all gitops functionality.
This is the main interface injected into publishing and validation workflows.

Production implementations of operations interfaces.

Provides real git, npm, fs, and build operations for production use.
For interface definitions and dependency injection pattern, see `operations.ts`.

Formats data and outputs to file or stdout based on options.

Supports three formats:
- stdout: Uses logger for colored/styled output (cannot use with `--outfile`)
- json: Stringified JSON
- markdown: Formatted markdown text

props.name: repo

declarations.name: DEFAULT_REPOS_DIR
declarations.docComment:
Default repos directory relative to gitops config file.
Resolves to the parent of the directory with the config
(e.g., `~/dev/repo/gitops.config.ts` resolves to `~/dev/`).

declarations.name: run_preflight_checks
declarations.docComment:
Validates all requirements before publishing can proceed.

Performs comprehensive pre-flight validation:
- Clean workspaces (100% clean required - no uncommitted changes)
- Correct branch (usually main)
- Changesets present (unless `skip_changesets`=true)
- Builds successful (fail-fast to prevent broken state)
- Git remote reachability
- NPM authentication with username
- NPM registry connectivity

Build validation runs BEFORE any publishing to prevent the scenario where
version is bumped but build fails, leaving repo in broken state.
declarations.typeSignature: ({ repos, preflight_options, git_ops, npm_ops, build_ops, changeset_ops, }: RunPreflightChecksOptions): Promise<PreflightResult>
declarations.returnDescription: result with `ok`=false if any errors, plus warnings and detailed status

declarations.name: PublishGate
declarations.docComment: What the task should do with a generated plan before executing the cascade.

members.name: wetrun
members.docComment: A real publish (`--wetrun`); a dry run never prompts.

members.name: show_plan
members.docComment: Show the plan and confirm (`plan`); `--no-plan` skips the prompt.

declarations.name: decide_publish_gate
declarations.docComment:
Decides whether a publish run must block, prompt for confirmation, or proceed without
prompting, from the pre-execution inputs.

- `blocked`: a real publish whose plan has errors — fail loud before prompting. The executor
  enforces this too, so `--no-plan` can't bypass the gate; this branch only avoids prompting
  for (and printing the "this will publish" banner of) a plan that can't run.
- `confirm`: a real publish that shows its plan — the user must confirm interactively.
- `proceed`: a dry run, or a `--no-plan` real publish — no prompt.

declarations.name: publish_run_failed
declarations.docComment:
Whether a finished run should exit non-zero: an unsuccessful result, or a fatal error thrown
out of the executor.

modules.moduleComment:
Pure decision logic for the `gitops_publish` task's interactive gating.

Extracted from `gitops_publish.task.ts` so the gating table is unit-testable without driving
readline or `process.exit`. The task keeps the IO — prompt, exit, logging — at its edge and
consults these for every branch.

declarations.name: PublishStepVia
declarations.docComment: How a package's version bump arises in the plan.

declarations.name: PublishStep
declarations.docComment: One ordered side-effect a wetrun would perform.

members.name: deploy
members.docComment: Include the deploy phase (the publisher only deploys with `--deploy`).

declarations.name: derive_publish_steps
declarations.docComment:
Derives the ordered side-effects a wetrun would perform from a frozen plan.

Reads only `publishing_order`, `version_changes`, and `dependency_updates` — the same data
`execute_publishing_plan` consumes, in the same order — so the preview reflects the real
pass. A dependency is only propagated to its dependents if it actually publishes this run.

declarations.name: format_publish_steps
declarations.docComment:
Formats steps as human-readable lines (one per step) for stdout and markdown output.
Returns a single placeholder line when there are no side effects.

modules.moduleComment:
Side-effect preview for a publishing plan.

`derive_publish_steps` linearizes a frozen `PublishingPlan` into the ordered side-effects
a `--wetrun` would perform, mirroring `execute_publishing_plan`'s pass so the preview and
the executor read the same plan data and can't drift. Pure — no side effects.

declarations.name: PublishingErrorCode
declarations.docComment:
Coarse triage classification for a failed package. Lets consumers branch on
failure kind without parsing the message.
declarations.typeSignature: ZodEnum<{ publish: "publish"; network: "network"; auth: "auth"; dependency: "dependency"; build: "build"; drift: "drift"; other: "other"; }>

declarations.name: PublishingRunSummary
declarations.docComment: Tallied outcome of a publishing run, derived from its events via `summarize_events`.
declarations.typeSignature: ZodObject<{ total: ZodNumber; published: ZodNumber; failed: ZodNumber; skipped: ZodNumber; duration: ZodNumber; }, $strict>

declarations.name: PublishingEvent
declarations.docComment:
A single structured event emitted during a publishing run. Tagged on `event` so the
union serializes as one self-describing JSON object per event (JSON-lines on the wire).
declarations.typeSignature: ZodDiscriminatedUnion<[ZodObject<{ event: ZodLiteral<"run_started">; wetrun: ZodBoolean; total: ZodNumber; }, $strict>, ZodObject<{ event: ZodLiteral<"package_skipped">; name: ZodString; reason: ZodString; }, $strict>, ... 7 more ..., ZodObject<...>], "event">

declarations.name: summarize_events
declarations.docComment:
Derives a run summary from the captured event list — the single canonical path from
events to summary, so the `run_finished` summary always agrees with the stream.
Call before emitting `run_finished` (which is not itself counted).
declarations.typeSignature: (events: ({ event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more ... | { ...; })[], duration: number): { ...; }

parameters.name: events
parameters.type: ({ event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 mor...
parameters.description: the events captured so far this run

declarations.returnType: { total: number; published: number; failed: number; skipped: number; duration: number; }

modules.moduleComment:
Structured events for multi-repo publishing.

Publishing emits a stream of tagged events alongside its human-readable logging,
so machine consumers (CI, dashboards) can follow a run step by step. Every run
opens with a `run_started` event carrying `wetrun`: when `false`, the run is a dry
run and every `package_completed` is a prediction (its `commit` is `'simulated'`)
rather than an applied change. A run's `run_finished` summary is derived from the
same event list via `summarize_events`, so the stream and the summary never drift.

Events are consumed through a `PublishingEventHandler` sink (see
`publishing_event_handler.ts`).

declarations.name: PublishingEventHandler
declarations.docComment: A sink for publishing events.

declarations.name: CapturingEventHandler
declarations.docComment: A `capture_handler` also exposes the events it has collected.

declarations.name: null_handler
declarations.docComment: Drops every event. The default when no handler is supplied.

declarations.name: capture_handler
declarations.docComment: Collects events in memory. Used to build the run report and in tests.

declarations.name: stdout_handler
declarations.docComment:
Writes each event as one JSON object per line (JSON-lines) to `process.stdout`.
Write failures are swallowed — the stream is observability, not control flow.

declarations.name: multi_handler
declarations.docComment: Fans an event out to every handler in order.

declarations.name: masking_handler
declarations.docComment: Wraps a handler, masking secrets in each event's string fields before forwarding.
declarations.typeSignature: (inner: PublishingEventHandler, mask?: (event: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { ...; } | ... 6 more ... | { ...; }) => { ...; } | ... 8 more ... | { ...; }): PublishingEventHandler

parameters.name: inner
parameters.description: the handler to forward masked events to

parameters.name: mask
parameters.type: (event: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | .....

declarations.name: redact_secrets
declarations.docComment: Redacts known secret shapes from a string.

declarations.name: mask_secrets
declarations.docComment: Returns a copy of the event with secrets redacted from its string-valued fields.
declarations.typeSignature: (event: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more ... | { ...; }): { ...; } | ... 8 more ... | { ...; }

parameters.name: event
parameters.type: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more...

declarations.returnType: { event: "run_started"; wetrun: boolean; total: number; } | { event: "package_skipped"; name: string; reason: string; } | { event: "package_completed"; name: string; old_version: string; new_version: string; bump_type: "major" | ... 1 more ... | "patch"; breaking: boolean; commit: string; tag: string; } | ... 6 more...

modules.moduleComment:
Composable sinks for the publishing event stream.

A `PublishingEventHandler` is anything that can receive a `PublishingEvent`. Handlers
compose: `multi_handler` fans out, `masking_handler` redacts secrets then forwards.
Emission is best-effort and synchronous — an observability sink must never fail or
slow a run. The default sink is `null_handler` (drops everything).

members.name: files
members.typeSignature: Array<{filename: string; bump_type: BumpType; summary: string}>

declarations.name: generate_publishing_plan
declarations.docComment:
Generates a publishing plan showing what would happen during publishing.
Shows version changes, dependency updates, and breaking change cascades.
Uses fixed-point iteration to resolve transitive cascades.

declarations.name: calculate_dependency_updates
declarations.docComment:
Calculates all dependency updates between packages based on predicted versions.

Iterates through all repos, checking prod, peer, and dev dependencies to find
which packages will need dependency version bumps after publishing.

Also tracks "breaking cascades" - when a breaking change propagates to dependents.
declarations.typeSignature: (repos: LocalRepo[], predicted_versions: Map<string, string>, breaking_packages: Set<string>): { dependency_updates: DependencyUpdate[]; breaking_cascades: Map<...>; }

declarations.name: get_required_bump_for_dependencies
declarations.docComment:
Determines the required bump type for a package based on its dependency updates.

Returns null if no prod/peer dependency updates, otherwise returns the minimum
required bump type (major for breaking deps, patch otherwise).

Respects pre-1.0 semver conventions (minor for breaking in 0.x).
declarations.typeSignature: (repo: LocalRepo, dependency_updates: DependencyUpdate[], breaking_packages: Set<string>): BumpType | null

Logging and formatting functions for publishing plans.

Includes both regular plan output and verbose diagnostic sections.

`package_json` is the repo's full `package.json`, carried alongside
